description:
You are the on-call system administrator.  
  1. The inventory file **/home/user/servers/hosts.lst** already exists and contains one hostname per line.  
  2. Your manager wants a quick, machine-readable tally of how many hosts are in that file.  
  3. In one single shell command (you may use pipes, redirection, or subshells, but everything must be typed on a single logical command line) produce that tally and write it to **/home/user/servers/hosts.count**.  

  VERY IMPORTANT – output format that will be automatically checked:  
  • /home/user/servers/hosts.count must contain **only** the integer count, expressed in base-10 ASCII digits, followed by a single trailing newline (no additional spaces, words, or blank lines).  
  • Example of the correct format if the count were 42:  
    42⏎  

  No other files may be created or modified.  
  Do NOT display the count on the terminal; only write it to the file.  
  You do not need sudo privileges; simply operate as the regular user.  
  Reminder: everything must be accomplished with one compound command entered on a single line of the terminal.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing setup -->
  Directory /home/user/servers already exists and is writable by the current user.

  File: /home/user/servers/hosts.lst  
  Exact content (7 lines, each line terminated by a single LF):
    host-a
    host-b
    host-c
    host-d
    host-e
    host-f
    host-g

  <!-- Verification criteria -->
  After successful completion the file /home/user/servers/hosts.count must exist and have the following exact byte sequence (including final newline, hexadecimal 0x0A):
    7\n
name: task_000000_403ec79b
